question: Will Sam Altman become more cautious about AGI/ASI development after having a child?
reference_class: Subjective Manifold-style markets on "public figure observably reverses a long-held public posture within ~18 months"
reasoning: Altman's son was born Feb 2025; the observable record since is uniformly toward LESS caution, not more. He explicitly rejected the question's mechanism on the record (Fortune, Aug 2025): "People say, 'Oh, I'm glad you have a kid because now you won't do something to destroy the world.' I was really set on not doing that before. Didn't need the kid." Revealed actions post-birth: "The Gentle Singularity" essay (Jun 2025) reframing takeoff as "gradual and manageable"; PBC conversion with personal equity (Oct 2025); superalignment/safety teams dissolved with researchers departing over commercialization; Feb 2026 prediction of superintelligence "within a couple of years"; May 2026 walk-back of his own AI-jobs-apocalypse warnings; Apr 2026 policy push critics call "regulatory nihilism" cover. The one YES-adjacent item — his Jul 2026 FT op-ed proposing a US-led IAEA-style forum to "guard against... unsafe racing" — is continuous with his 2023 IAEA proposal and framed by coverage as competitive/incumbent positioning, not developmental restraint. Fatherhood quotes ("totally rewired my priorities") concern outlook/optimism, and a generous resolver could cherry-pick them, which is most of the residual YES mass alongside unknown creator idiosyncrasy on a subjective market. Only ~20 days remain to resolution; a visible cautious pivot in that window is very unlikely. Status quo (NO) dominates; evidence is strong and one-sided, so I discriminate below the base rate.
what_would_change_my_mind: The market creator's stated conditions turn out to be much weaker than 'observably more cautious posture' (e.g., resolves YES if Altman merely says fatherhood changed how he thinks about his work)
An explicit Altman statement before Jul 31, 2026 attributing increased risk concern or a development slowdown to having kids
OpenAI announcing a safety-driven pause, major model-release delay, or restored superalignment-scale compute commitment framed around Altman's personal shift
Evidence the creator has a history of resolving similar subjective markets YES on thin, vibes-based evidence

question: [ACX 2026] Will the winner of the 2026 FIFA World Cup be a country that has never won before?
reference_class: World Cup editions won by a first-time winner (5/22 all-time, 1/11 since 1970), conditioned on live knockout-bracket composition with 6 teams left of which only Norway and Switzerland are non-winners
reasoning: Naive: only 8 nations have ever won; new winner in 1 of last 11 editions (~9%). But at T-8 days the live bracket dominates. SF1 is set as France vs Spain (both former champions) — the entire YES mass sits on Norway or Switzerland winning from the bottom half. Norway-England QF is LIVE, 1-1 at ~76' (Schjelderup 36', Bellingham 45+2'); pre-match England was ~62/38 favorite (Opta), conditional on level at 76' I put Norway ~44% to advance (England depleted backline but better bench and Pickford in a shootout). Argentina-Switzerland kicks off tonight: Argentina fully fit, Messi 8 goals, 7-0-0 H2H, but shaky knockouts (ET vs Cape Verde, comeback vs Egypt); Switzerland ~22% with Manzambi a knee doubt. Then a would-be first-time winner must beat the other bottom-half survivor (Norway ~39% blended, Swiss ~37%) AND France/Spain in the final (Norway ~27% — France beat Norway in the group; Switzerland ~20%). P(Norway title) ≈ 0.44×0.39×0.27 ≈ 4.7%; P(Swiss title) ≈ 0.22×0.37×0.20 ≈ 1.6%; total ≈ 6.3%. Key structure: ~44% chance both England and Argentina advance tonight, making YES near-impossible; Norway winning tonight roughly doubles it to ~11-13%. Set 0.065.
what_would_change_my_mind: Norway beats England tonight (raise to ~11-13%); England wins (drop to ~1.5-2%, Switzerland-only path)
Switzerland upsets Argentina tonight (roughly doubles the surviving path's weight); Argentina wins and England wins → drop to ~0.001-0.005
Haaland injury/illness recurrence or a red card in the remaining minutes/ET of the live match
Late team news that Manzambi is fit and starts for Switzerland, or visible Argentina fatigue/injury from tonight's match
Evidence France-Spain winner emerges badly injured/suspended ahead of the July 19 final, softening the final hurdle for a first-time finalist

question: Will Anthropic or Mistral AI sign the European Commission's AI Pact before September 1, 2026?
reference_class: Specific named companies that publicly declined a voluntary EU pledge for 22 months, joining within a ~7-week window (AI Pact accretion: ~100 additions in 21 months from a pool of thousands of eligible firms)
reasoning: Verified the resolution source directly: the Commission's official AI Pact signatory list (digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/ai-pact, updated 2026-06-09) shows neither company — checked exhaustively (A: Aleph Alpha, Amazon, Anthology but no Anthropic; M: Microsoft, Mastercard but no Mistral). Both signed the separate GPAI Code of Practice in July 2025, which is the instrument that matters for GPAI providers and removes most of the incentive to also sign the Pact; the question must not be conflated with the CoP. Both have had 22 months of open enrollment and conspicuously declined — revealed preference. Current incentives point further away: the May 2026 digital-omnibus deal defers high-risk obligations to Dec 2027, deflating the Pact's purpose; Anthropic just dropped its flagship safety pledge (TIME, Feb 2026) and is consumed by the June 2026 US export-control cutoff of Mythos/Fable to the EU; Mistral's European policy playbook doesn't even mention the Pact and it just won the AI Act timeline relief it lobbied for. Pact accretion runs ~5 mostly-SME additions/month from a pool of thousands, so the naive two-company/7-week hazard is ~1-2%. I add a small premium for the volatile EU-Anthropic courtship (Austria hosting proposal, partnership talks, possible symbolic gestures around the Aug 2 AI Act anniversary) where a cheap goodwill signature is conceivable, landing at 3%.
what_would_change_my_mind: Anthropic or Mistral announces intent to sign the AI Pact, or the official EC signatory list adds either name before 2026-09-01
An EU-Anthropic partnership/hosting deal (e.g., the Austria proposal) that packages voluntary commitments as an AI Pact signature
A Commission signing ceremony or second-wave pledge drive announced around the Aug 2, 2026 AI Act anniversary targeting GPAI providers
Mistral making a sovereignty-branding regulatory gesture amid the post-export-control 'European champion' push
Evidence the question's resolver would (incorrectly) count the GPAI Code of Practice signatory list as the AI Pact list — both companies are on that one

question: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of July? [Polymarket]
reference_class: Maritime chokepoint traffic fully normalizing within ~3 weeks while under active military attack/blockade (Red Sea 2023-25, Suez closures, 1984-88 Tanker War, this crisis's own two failed-ceasefire recoveries)
reasoning: Status quo: strait effectively closed — 11 transits Jul 10 vs ~88-140/day pre-war baseline; only sanctioned/Iranian vessels moving; IMO advising avoidance; war-risk premiums ~5% of hull value. Ceasefire collapsed Jul 6-8 (Iran attacked 3 tankers, US resumed strikes, Iran hit US assets in 5 countries), so YES requires a brand-new deal AND full traffic normalization in 20 days. Key mechanism check: after the Jun 17 MoU, the best recovery this crisis ever achieved was ~40-43 transits/day (~35-50% of normal) after 2.5 weeks, with premiums bottoming at 2% (8x pre-war) — so even an immediate deal cannot plausibly reach "normal" by Jul 31; Goldman's pre-collapse optimistic case was only ~70% of pre-war Hormuz flows by end-July. Diplomacy (Muscat talks, Oman two-route proposal, Qatar/Pakistan mediation) is deadlocked on the core issue: Iran rejects the routes plan, US demands Iran publicly declare the strait open; hardline new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei and Trump are trading annihilation threats. Two ceasefires already failed (Apr 8, Jun 17), destroying shipper/insurer confidence — third-time recoveries are slower, not faster. Outside view (Red Sea 2023-25, Tanker War, Suez): chokepoints under active attack normalize over months-years, never ~3 weeks from a live shooting phase. Residual YES mass comes almost entirely from criterion risk — if the mirrored contract defines "normal" leniently (e.g., return to pre-Jul-7 ceasefire levels, ~15% weight, ~12-15% conditional) rather than pre-war baseline (~85% weight, ~1.5% conditional). Weighted ≈ 3%.
what_would_change_my_mind: A comprehensive US-Iran deal announced within ~5 days in which Iran publicly declares the strait open to all shipping and attacks verifiably stop
Daily transit counts recovering past ~60-70/day (well above the previous ceasefire peak of ~43) by ~July 20 on tracker data
War-risk premiums collapsing toward pre-war fractions of a percent and IMO lifting its avoidance advisory before mid-July
Discovery that the mirrored Polymarket criterion defines 'normal' as return to pre-July-7 ceasefire traffic levels (~40/day) rather than pre-war baseline
Evidence of mass unescorted commercial transits resuming on the main routes (not just Omani convoys or sanctioned Iranian vessels)

question: Perfect score achieved by an AI model in the International Math Olympiad (IMO) 2026?
reference_class: Frontier AI real-time attempts at full IMO problem sets (2024: 28/42; 2025: 35/42 with universal P6 failure), conditioned on one year of documented olympiad-math capability gain
reasoning: Naive: never done (0/2 real-time frontier attempts); 2025 both leaders hit exactly 35/42 with all AIs scoring 0 on P6 (solved by 6/~630 humans) — base rate for first-ever perfect ~25%. Research moves it up sharply: DeepMind's Jan-2026 Deep Think "significantly outperforms" the IMO-gold version (~90% IMO-ProofBench Advanced, 4 open Erdős problems solved autonomously), GPT-5.4 at 95.2 USAMO-2026, DeepSeekMath-V2/Seed-Prover at gold level, and July-2026 internal models are ~6 months beyond all of that. Attempt intensity is maximal: Shanghai venue mobilizes Chinese labs, OpenAI has a year of prep vs a 2-month sprint that got 35, and only 42 is newsworthy now. Against: the binding constraint is one adversarially novel hard problem — P6-2025 stumped every lab simultaneously and stayed autonomously unsolved ~5 months (Dec-2025 crack required human orchestration of GPT-5.1+Gemini 3 Pro); the jury knows AI attempts and can tilt P3/P6 toward AI-hard combinatorial constructions; perfect also requires zero dropped points across six write-ups. Decomposition: P(hardest problem AI-tractable) ~0.65 × P(one system clean-sweeps all 6 | tractable) ~0.87 × P(credible verification by Jul 31 | achieved) ~0.93 (ceremony Jul 20; 2025 announcements came 0-8 days post-ceremony) ≈ 0.53-0.58; capability momentum being one-sided puts me at the top of that band. Blind mode kept: a stray snippet showed a market-implied number for the separate gold question; discarded, not used.
what_would_change_my_mind: Post-paper (Jul 15-16) leaks or coordinator chatter that the 2026 P3/P6 are novel combinatorial constructions of the P6-2025 flavor — would drop me toward 0.35
A lab announcement before/at the closing ceremony claiming all six solved — would push above 0.9 pending grading of full 7s
Evidence that DeepMind/OpenAI internal models autonomously (no human orchestration) solved IMO-2025 P6 or equivalent fresh hard combinatorics — would push to ~0.7 pre-contest

Signals that labs agreed to delay announcements beyond ~1 week post-ceremony or that the market creator requires official IMO certification (only DeepMind has that channel) — timing/verification haircut grows, drop ~5-10 points
Multiple perfect human scores again but jury statements about deliberately AI-resistant selection — modest drop

question: Will Mitch McConnell make a verifiable, live public appearance before August 3, 2026?
reference_class: Senators aged 80+ mid-way through a serious multi-week hospitalization: probability of a first live public appearance within the next 3 weeks while still inpatient at the 4-week mark (McConnell 2023, Feinstein 2023, Fetterman 2023 analogs)
reasoning: Naive answer (sitting senator appears publicly in any 3-week window) is ~0.99 — but research flips it. McConnell, 84, was found unconscious at home June 14 (EMS dispatch audio mentions "cardiac arrest"), has been continuously hospitalized 27 days, and has made zero public appearances or statements since (last seen ~June 6-11, pre-window). Despite maximal pressure — brain-death rumors, a fake proof-of-life image, Gov. Beshear's public letter, MAGA media demands — his office has released no photo, video, or audio in four weeks; the revealed action (not even a controlled photo, which is strictly easier than a live appearance) strongly implies he is not presentable. Positive signals exist but are moves by aligned actors: Thune/Barrasso/Jennings describe substantive phone calls (cognition likely intact), a source says he's "alive and undergoing rehab in the hospital," and Jennings claims he's "preparing to return to work" — spin from a loyalist, no date given. Precedents for elderly senators' door-to-first-appearance after serious hospitalization: McConnell 2023 concussion ~6 weeks (from a 5-day hospitalization), Feinstein ~10 weeks, Fetterman ~8 weeks — all exceed the 21 days remaining, and here he is STILL inpatient at week 4, so the clock effectively starts later. Kentucky's Aug 3 vacancy-law deadline incentivizes his team to ride out July in silence rather than resign or prove capacity. YES paths: dramatic recovery → Senate return during the July 13–Aug 7 work period (he chairs defense approps amid July markups) ~8%; staged live appearance/press-witnessed discharge ~6-7% (office's track record is silence-until-fully-ready); small resolver-leniency add. Death before Aug 1 (~5%) is folded into NO. Net ~0.15.
what_would_change_my_mind: Announced hospital discharge with a stated return-to-Senate date before Aug 1, or reporting he will attend a specific July vote/markup (raise sharply, to 0.5+)
Office releases an authentic recent photo/video showing him alert and presentable — signals a live appearance is imminent (raise to ~0.3-0.4)
Credible reporting he attended or will attend any press-witnessed event, even a brief driveway/hospital-exit appearance (raise toward 0.8+)
News of death, resignation, transfer to long-term care, or an 'expected back in September/fall' timeline (drop to ≤0.05)
Evidence the question resolver counts pre-June-14 appearances or non-live released footage as qualifying (raise substantially on interpretation alone)

question: Title of the movie spoken within "The Odyssey"?
reference_class: Christopher Nolan films with thematic common-noun titles — spoken title-drop rate (~70%), anchored against 36.5% all-film base
reasoning: Film premiered Jul 6 but full review embargo lifts Jul 15, so outcome is unpublicized — this is a genuine model-based call. General title-drop base rate is 36.5% (titledrops.net, 74k films), but Nolan's personal rate is ~70% (Inception, Tenet, Prestige, Dark Knight, Oppenheimer, Interstellar all drop the title), and one-word thematic common-noun titles are his strongest category. Pro-YES: Nolan confirmed aggressively modern colloquial dialogue ("daddy" line), removing the anachronism barrier to "odyssey" as a common noun; Travis Scott plays a bard whose oral retelling of Odysseus's story Nolan calls "such an important part of the film" — a built-in frame for naming the tale; ~3-hr runtime. Pro-NO: "odyssey" derives from Odysseus and shouldn't exist in-world, so a drop requires deliberate flourish; no "odyssey" appears in any quoted dialogue across teaser, TV spot, May trailer, and final trailer (Nolan marketing historically showcases title drops — Tenet/Inception trailers did), though only ~12 promo lines were verifiable; comparable epics (Troy, The Return) avoided naming the source poem. Resolution ambiguity (does bare "odyssey" or a credits-song lyric count?) adds symmetric noise. Netting Nolan's strong habit and the modern-idiom register against the etymological awkwardness and absent trailer evidence: modestly above even.
what_would_change_my_mind: Full reviews after the July 15 embargo quoting any line containing 'odyssey' (would push to 0.95+) or explicitly noting the title is never spoken (push to <0.10)
A premiere attendee/Letterboxd/Reddit spoiler post confirming or denying a title drop
The market creator's stated conditions being stricter (exact phrase 'The Odyssey' in dialogue only) or looser (credits-song lyrics count) than assumed
Publication of the 'When I'm Home' lyrics containing 'odyssey' combined with evidence the song plays diegetically before credits
A full transcript of the final trailer revealing a spoken 'odyssey' line
